theory.intro: A side-by-side column graph shows two groups for each category, drawn as two bars next to each other. A legend tells you which shade is which group.
facts: Each category has **two bars** — one for each group, in different shades.
Use the **legend** to know which shade stands for which group.
Read each bar's height against the same vertical scale to compare the two groups.
steps: Find the category along the bottom.
Use the legend to pick the correct shaded bar.
Read its height off the vertical scale.

foundation.q: The graph compares boys and girls in two sports. How many boys play soccer? (dark = boys, light = girls)
Using the same graph, how many girls play netball?
Using the sports graph, in soccer are there more boys or girls?
The graph compares two days of fruit sales. How many apples on Monday? (dark = Mon, light = Tue)
Using the fruit graph, how many pears on Tuesday?
The graph compares two classes' scores. What did class A score in maths? (dark = A, light = B)
Using the scores graph, what did class B score in science?
The graph compares cold and warm month visitors. In June, which group is taller? (dark = cold, light = warm)
The graph compares two weeks of recycling. How many bottles in week $1$? (dark = wk1, light = wk2)
Using the recycling graph, how many cans in week $2$?

development.q: The graph compares boys and girls. In soccer, how many more boys than girls? (dark = boys, light = girls)
Using the same graph, how many children chose dance in total?
The graph compares two shops' sales (dark = shop A, light = shop B). For which item are sales equal?
The graph compares maths and reading scores for two terms (dark = T1, light = T2). What was the total maths score across both terms?
Using the term scores graph, did reading improve from term $1$ to term $2$?
The graph compares two teams' goals over two games (dark = red, light = blue). Which team scored more in total?
The graph compares rainfall in two towns (dark = town X, light = town Y). In which month is the gap between towns largest?
The graph compares two years' attendance (dark = 2024, light = 2025). What was the total in 2025 across both events?
The graph compares juice and water chosen by two classes (dark = 3A, light = 3B). How many chose water altogether?
Using the drinks graph, which class chose more juice, and by how many?

mastery.q: The graph compares boys and girls in two activities (dark = boys, light = girls). How many children are shown in total?
Using the same graph, what fraction of all children chose art?
A side-by-side graph shows two terms' scores (dark = T1, light = T2). The reading T2 bar is missing, but reading rose by $20$ from its T1 value of $30$. What is the T2 value?
A side-by-side graph compares two shops over two days (dark = A, light = B). Shop A sold $30$ then $50$; shop B sold $40$ then $20$. Which shop sold more across both days?
A side-by-side graph shows two teams (dark = home, light = away). Home scored $20, 40$; away scored $30, 30$. What was the average away score?
A side-by-side graph compares pets in two classes (dark = 3A, light = 3B). Reasoning: if 3A has $20$ pets total and 3B has $30$, which class has more pets per child if both have $10$ children?
A side-by-side graph compares two months (dark = sunny days, light = rainy days). June: $20$ sunny, $10$ rainy. What percentage of June days were rainy (out of $30$)?
A side-by-side graph compares two camps (dark = camp A, light = camp B). Across three activities A scored $4, 2, 3$ and B scored $3, 5, 1$. Which camp had the higher total?
A side-by-side graph compares before and after a campaign (dark = before, light = after). Litter dropped from $50$ to $20$. What was the percentage decrease?
Explain why a side-by-side column graph is better than a single column graph for comparing boys' and girls' choices.
mastery.a: it shows both groups for each category next to each other
